message.content:
Below is an **exhaustive, testable checklist** for X/Twitter Cards markup, organized by card type and the shared requirements you asked for. I’m using the X/Twitter developer documentation as the primary authority for the card types, required tags, fallback behavior, and the documented placement in `<head>`; where the exact X docs URL is not available in the search results, I mark that as *not provided in the search results* rather than inventing it. The X developer docs generally describe Twitter Cards as metadata placed in the `<head>` and note that when specific Twitter card tags are absent, X can use **Open Graph** metadata as fallback for some values.[7][8]

### Shared checks for all card types

| Tag / check | What it verifies | Exact X developer documentation URL |
|---|---|---|
| `\<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary">`, `summary_large_image`, `player`, or `app` | Verifies the page declares the card **type** X should render.[1][7][8] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| Place all card meta tags in the document `<head>` | Verifies the markup is in the documented location X crawlers read for Cards.[2][4] | *Exact X docs URL not provided in the search results* |
| Open Graph fallback metadata present when Twitter-specific tags are missing | Verifies the page still exposes social metadata X can reuse for preview generation when Twitter card fields are incomplete.[4][7][8] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |

### `summary` card checks

| Tag / check | What it verifies | Exact X developer documentation URL |
|---|---|---|
| `twitter:card = summary` | Verifies the card is explicitly configured as a **Summary Card**.[1][4] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:title` | Verifies the summary card has a title string for the preview.[2][4] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:description` | Verifies the summary card has descriptive preview copy.[2][4] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:image` | Verifies the summary card has an image URL to display.[2][4] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:image:alt` | Verifies the image includes accessible alternate text for the card image.[7][8] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:site` | Verifies the publisher/site attribution is declared.[2][4][7] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |

### `summary_large_image` card checks

| Tag / check | What it verifies | Exact X developer documentation URL |
|---|---|---|
| `twitter:card = summary_large_image` | Verifies the page requests the **large image summary** card variant.[2][4][6] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:title` | Verifies the card has a title for the large-image preview.[2][4] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:description` | Verifies the card has supporting descriptive text.[2][4] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:image` | Verifies the large-image card includes the preview image URL.[2][4] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:image:alt` | Verifies the preview image has alternate text, if provided by the implementation.[7][8] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:site` | Verifies publisher attribution for the card.[2][4][7] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |

### `player` card checks

| Tag / check | What it verifies | Exact X developer documentation URL |
|---|---|---|
| `twitter:card = player` | Verifies the page requests a **Player Card**.[7][8] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:player` | Verifies the embeddable player URL is supplied.[7][8] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:player:width` | Verifies the player width is declared for rendering.[5][6][7] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:player:height` | Verifies the player height is declared for rendering.[5][6][7] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:player:stream` | Verifies the media stream URL is supplied for player playback.[7][8] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |

### `app` card checks

| Tag / check | What it verifies | Exact X developer documentation URL |
|---|---|---|
| `twitter:card = app` | Verifies the page requests an **App Card**.[1][6][7] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:app:id:iphone` | Verifies the iPhone App Store app ID is present.[6] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:app:id:ipad` | Verifies the iPad App Store app ID is present, if the iPad variant is supported.[6] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:app:id:googleplay` | Verifies the Google Play app ID is present.[6] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:app:name` | Verifies the app name is declared for the app preview.[6] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:app:url` | Verifies the app URL/deep link is declared for the app preview.[6] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |

### Fallback to Open Graph tags

| Tag / check | What it verifies | Exact X developer documentation URL |
|---|---|---|
| `og:title` | Verifies title fallback metadata exists when a Twitter title is absent.[4] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `og:description` | Verifies description fallback metadata exists when `twitter:description` is absent.[4] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `og:image` | Verifies image fallback metadata exists when `twitter:image` is absent.[4] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `og:url` | Verifies canonical URL fallback metadata exists for the page identity.[4] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |

### Image size and format constraints

| Tag / check | What it verifies | Exact X developer documentation URL |
|---|---|---|
| `twitter:image` file type is supported | Verifies the image uses a supported format; search results report **JPG, PNG, WEBP, and GIF** as acceptable.[2] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:image` file size is within limits | Verifies the image is not too large; search results report a **maximum of 5 MB**.[2][4] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |
| `twitter:image` dimensions are appropriate for the card type | Verifies the image dimensions match the intended card presentation; search results report **minimum 144 × 144** for Summary and **minimum 300 × 157** for Summary with Large Image.[2] | `https://devcommunity.x.com/c/publisher/cards/8` |

### Important source note

- The search results include **X Developer Community** pages that discuss card markup and card basics, but they do **not** include the canonical, current X documentation page that lists every tag for every card type.[7][8]
- Because of that, I have used the X developer community cards hub as the exact X URL available in the results, and I have not invented a missing docs URL.
- If you want, I can next convert this into a **QA test matrix** with columns for *Required / Optional / Validation method / Expected failure mode / Example pass-fail cases* using only X, Schema.org, Open Graph, and web.dev-style authoritative sources.
